califOrnia
Fair Political
Practices Comrnission
June 20, 1990
ceorge Johnson
Lorflpoc Realty
410 E. Ocean Avenue
Lompoc, CA 93456
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our Fi,l,e No. I-9 0-3 21
Dear Mr. Johnson:
This is in response to your letter of May 1, 1990 requesting
advice from this agency as a potential candidate for elective of-
fice in the city of Lompoc. The Fair Political Practices commis-
sion provides advice with resPect to the provisions of the Pol-iti-
ca1 nlform Act (the rrR"grrl .1 This letter therefore addresses your
inquiry only with respect to the Act; you are advised to contact
your city attorney's office for further information concerning
other provisions.
OUESTTON
You have inquired whether you may run for more than one city
elective office in the November 1990 election.
CONCLUSION
The Act does not prohibit you from seeking electi-on or bej-ng
elected to nul-tiple offices. 
However, because other lega1 provi-
sions could inpact on your sj-tuation, you should contact your city
attorney as to those matters, as discussed in this letter.
FACTS
In the November l-990 election the City of Lompoc will be con-
ducting an election for the office of mayor and for three-city
councii offices. 
There will actualty be three separate elections:
(1) the mayoral election, for a two year term; (2) two council
ofii""=, 
eich for four year tenns; and (3) a special election to
fill 
the remaining two years of a council office which has become
1 Go.r"rtr"nt code Sections 81,OOO-91015. AII statutory references
are to the Government code unless otherwise indi.cated. commi-ssion
regulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations . Section
ia5oo, et seo-.- A1I references to regulations are to Title 2, Di-
visi.on 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 . Sacramento CA 95804-0807 . (916)322'5660


our FiLe No. r-9 0 -3 21
Paqe 2
vacant. You are interested in runnj.ng in all three separate
elections, but have been informed by the city clerk that such was
not possible.
ANALYSI S
There are no provisions of the Act which either precl-ude an
individual from seeking more than one elective office j-n a singJ.e
election, or from serving in nore than one elective office at any
one time. The Act's campaign, disclosure, and conflict of inter-
est provisions wouLd apply to each candidacy/ office sought and
held:2
Moreover, since salary fron a Ioca1 government agency is not
rrincometr under the Act (section 82030(b)(2)), running for or hold-
ing multiple l-oca1 offices woul-d not alone qive rise to a conflict
of interest under the Act. 
(Sections 87100, 87103(c).)
The advice provided by the Fair Political Practices Commis-
sj-on is limited to the provisions of the Act. other lega1 provi-
sions, such as Government Code Section l-090, the general doctrine
of incompatible offices, various ELection Code sections, or any
loca1 ordinances, all may have some bearing on your situation but
are beyond the scope of this letter. 
You should contact your city
attorney for advice as to these matters.
I trust this letter has provided you with the guj-dance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this mat-
ter, pl-ease contact ne at (9L6) 322-59oL-
S incere Iy,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral counsel
